title: rubus rosifolius subsp. sumatranus
intro: Rubus rosifolius subsp. sumatranus, also known as the Sumatran raspberry, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the rose family that is native to the tropical forests of Sumatra in Indonesia. It is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which have been used to treat various health conditions. The plant is believed to have several health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving pain, and aiding in the treatment of skin conditions such as eczema and acne. Rubus rosifolius subsp. sumatranus is often prepared as a herbal tea or infused oil, which can be used to promote wound healing and reduce inflammation.
